subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Per Hero
- **5 custom dice** — unique faces per hero showing ability icons and numbers
- **1 hero board** — shows all abilities, CP costs, upgrade slots, and health tracker
- **Ability cards** — specific to each hero, used to upgrade abilities
- **Status effect tokens** — for applying conditions to opponents
- **1 health dial or tracker** — typically starts at 50 HP (Season 2) or varies
- **Combat Points (CP) tracker** — tracks available CP

#### Shared Components
- **Status effect reference cards**
- **Turn order cards** (for multiplayer)

#### Available Heroes (Season 1)
- Barbarian, Moon Elf, Pyromancer, Shadow Thief, Monk, Paladin

#### Available Heroes (Season 2)
- Gunslinger, Samurai, Tactician, Huntress, Seraph, Vampire Lord

Note: Any hero from any season can fight any other hero.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **No shared board** — Dice Throne has no map or shared board. Each player has their own hero board.
2. **Hero board layout** — Shows:
   - **Health tracker** — along the edge, starting at ~50 HP.
   - **Ability grid** — 5-6 abilities shown with dice requirements and damage/effects.
   - **Upgrade slots** — empty slots that can be filled with ability cards to enhance abilities.
   - **CP tracker** — tracks Combat Points available.
3. **Play area** — Each player needs space for their dice, cards, and tokens.
4. **Central area** — Place status effect tokens within reach of all players.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player
- **Choose a hero** — Each hero plays completely differently.
- **Take hero components** — Hero board, 5 custom dice, ability cards, status tokens.
- **Set health** — Set health dial to starting value (typically 50 HP in Season 2, varies in Season 1).
- **Set CP** — Start with 1 Combat Point.
- **Draw cards** — Draw 4 cards from your hero's ability deck.
- **No upgrades yet** — All upgrade slots on the hero board start empty.

#### Card Hand
- Maximum hand size is **6 cards**.
- Cards can be played to upgrade abilities, modify dice, or apply effects.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **50 HP** (Season 2) or hero-specific starting health.
- **1 CP** to start.
- **4 cards** in hand.
- **Determine first player** randomly.
- **No upgrades** — Abilities start at base level.
- **Goal** — Reduce your opponent to 0 HP.
- **Timed variant** — In timed games, most remaining HP wins when time expires.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each turn follows these phases in order:

#### 1. Upkeep Phase
- Gain **1 CP** (Combat Point).
- Resolve any start-of-turn status effects (poison damage, burn, etc.).
- Draw 1 card (if below max hand size).

#### 2. Income Phase
- Gain CP based on your hero's income value and any modifiers.
- You may sell cards from hand for 1 CP each.

#### 3. Main Phase (Pre-Roll)
- Play cards to upgrade abilities (pay CP cost, place card in upgrade slot).
- Play Main Phase action cards.
- Activate certain hero-specific abilities.

#### 4. Offensive Roll Phase
- Roll all **5 dice**.
- You may re-roll any number of dice (up to **2 re-rolls**, Yahtzee-style).
- Play cards to modify dice results between rolls.
- After final roll, choose which ability to activate based on dice results.

#### 5. Targeting/Attack Phase
- Declare your activated ability against an opponent.
- Opponent may play defensive cards or abilities.
- Resolve damage and effects.

#### 6. Defensive Roll Phase (Opponent)
- Some heroes have defensive abilities triggered by specific dice results.
- The defender may roll dice to mitigate damage (if their hero has this ability).

#### 7. Discard Phase
- Discard down to max hand size if needed.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Dice Abilities
Each hero has **5-6 unique abilities** activated by specific dice combinations:
- **Small Straight** (1-2-3-4 or 2-3-4-5) — triggers one ability.
- **Large Straight** (1-2-3-4-5) — triggers a stronger ability.
- **Three of a Kind** — triggers an ability, damage often scales with the number.
- **Four of a Kind** — triggers a stronger version.
- **Five of a Kind** — ultimate ability, devastating damage or effect.
- **Specific combinations** — Each hero has custom dice faces that create unique combos.

#### Card Types
- **Upgrade cards** — Permanently enhance an ability (place in upgrade slot). Adds damage, effects, or lowers requirements.
- **Roll Modifier cards** — Change dice faces during the roll phase. Add +1 to a die, change a face, etc.
- **Main Phase cards** — Played before rolling for strategic effects.
- **Instant cards** — Can be played at specific timing windows.

#### Status Effects
- **Poison** — Damage at start of turn.
- **Burn** — Damage at start of turn (often stronger).
- **Blind** — Limits re-rolls.
- **Freeze** — Removes a die from your roll.
- **Shield** — Absorbs incoming damage.
- Various hero-specific statuses.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Yahtzee-Style Rolling
- Roll 5 dice, keep any, re-roll the rest.
- Up to **2 re-rolls** (3 total rolls).
- Cards can modify results between or after rolls.
- The tension is in deciding what ability to aim for and when to stop rolling.

#### Combat Points (CP)
- CP is the currency for upgrades, card effects, and some abilities.
- Gained each turn (1 base + income).
- Selling cards gives 1 CP each.
- Managing CP economy is crucial — upgrades cost CP but make abilities much stronger.

#### Upgrades
- Each ability has **1-2 upgrade slots**.
- Playing an upgrade card into a slot permanently enhances that ability for the rest of the game.
- Upgraded abilities deal more damage, apply status effects, or have reduced dice requirements.
- Upgrading early gives more value over the course of the game.

#### Asymmetry
- Each hero has completely different dice faces, abilities, and strategies.
- The Barbarian is straightforward and hits hard. The Shadow Thief steals CP and poisons. The Pyromancer burns. The Moon Elf is ranged and evasive.
- Matchup knowledge matters in competitive play.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
Reduce your opponent's health to **0 HP**. That player is eliminated.

#### Multiplayer
- In 3-6 player games, play as free-for-all or teams.
- **Last hero standing** wins in free-for-all.
- In team games, a team wins when all opposing heroes are eliminated.

#### Timed Variant
- Set a timer (e.g., 30 minutes).
- When time expires, the player with the **most remaining HP** wins.
- Useful for tournaments or quick games.

#### No Point Scoring
The game is purely elimination-based. There is no VP system.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Upgrade early** — Upgrades are permanent. Getting them down early means more value over the game.
- **Learn your hero's best ability** — Know which dice combo deals the most damage and aim for it.
- **Manage CP carefully** — Don't blow all CP on one big upgrade if you need cards to modify dice.
- **Apply status effects** — Poison and burn deal damage every turn. Early application maximizes total damage.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Chasing the ultimate ability** — Five of a Kind is rare. Don't waste re-rolls chasing it when a Three of a Kind is guaranteed damage.
- **Ignoring defense** — Some heroes have powerful defensive options. Saving cards for defense can be game-changing.
- **Hoarding cards** — Cards in hand do nothing. Play them for upgrades or dice modification.
- **Not selling cards** — Sometimes selling a useless card for 1 CP to fund an upgrade is the right play.
- **Forgetting status effects** — Track poison, burn, and other effects carefully. Missing upkeep damage is a common error.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Which ability to aim for** — After your first roll, decide whether to go for a safe ability or gamble on a stronger one.
- **When to stop re-rolling** — Sometimes your first roll is good enough. Don't risk losing a solid result.
- **Upgrade order** — Which ability to upgrade first depends on your hero and the matchup.
- **Cards for offense or defense** — Do you save modifier cards for your attack or hold them for defensive situations?
- **Target selection (multiplayer)** — In free-for-all, choosing who to attack is a political and strategic decision.
